summary: Implement tiered daily late-fee interest for overdue invoices (BR-INT-001)
description: As Accounts Receivable, we need INTCALC to apply a tiered daily interest rate to overdue invoices so that late fees scale with how overdue an invoice is. Rate tiers: 1-30 days overdue = 0.0150% per day; 31-60 days overdue = 0.0250% per day; 61+ days overdue = 0.0400% per day. Late fee = invoice amount * daily rate * days overdue (simple, non-compounding interest). Invoices with 0 or negative days overdue accrue no fee.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an invoice 15 days overdue, the daily rate applied is 0.000150
Given an invoice 45 days overdue, the daily rate applied is 0.000250
Given an invoice 90 days overdue, the daily rate applied is 0.000400
Given an invoice with 0 days overdue, late fee is 0.00
Late fee formula is invoice amount * daily rate * days overdue, rounded to 2 decimal places

summary: Cap late-fee interest at 25% of invoice amount (BR-INT-001 amendment)
description: Legal/Compliance requires a hard cap on late fees so that long-overdue invoices do not accrue unbounded interest. Amend INTCALC so the computed late fee never exceeds 25% of the original invoice amount, regardless of how many days overdue the invoice is. This caps applies after the tiered daily rate calculation in VPM-100.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an invoice amount of 1000.00 and a calculated late fee greater than 250.00, the applied late fee is capped at 250.00
Given an invoice amount of 1000.00 and a calculated late fee of 150.00 (under the cap), the applied late fee remains 150.00
The cap is exactly 25% (0.25) of INV-INVOICE-AMOUNT, rounded to 2 decimal places

summary: Late fee must round to 2 decimal places using standard rounding
description: Finance reported penny-level discrepancies when late fees were truncated instead of rounded. INTCALC's COMPUTE statements use ROUNDED for both the late fee and the cap calculation; the Java conversion must replicate standard half-up rounding to 2 decimal places to avoid reconciliation drift against legacy output.
acceptanceCriteria: Late fee values are rounded half-up to 2 decimal places
Principal-plus-fee total is computed from the rounded late fee, not the unrounded intermediate value
